Phoenician merchants stack cedar logs on stone quays while purple-dye workers rinse cloth. Galleys with painted eyes await cargo beneath Tyre's walls.
Reed boats cluster at Memphis dockside as workers roll grain amphorae toward barges. A stone obelisk lies secured on massive timber transports.
Knossos harbor shimmers with dolphin-prowed vessels. Traders inspect octopus-pattern jars while painted warehouses overlook the wide turquoise basin below.
Lothal's brick-lined dock fills at high tide. Merchants weigh carnelian beads on standardized scales beside ships bearing Mesopotamian seal-marked cargo.
Dilmun's pearl divers surface near moored vessels from Ur and Harappa. Bronze goods and timber exchange hands on Bahrain's bustling quayside.
Byblos stone harbor hosts Egyptian ships loading Lebanese cedar. Scribes tally timber lengths while Pharaoh's captains oversee the sacred wood's transport.
Early Carthage's anchorage teems with Phoenician trading vessels. Merchants unload African ivory and Spanish silver onto the young colony's expanding wharves.
Nile Delta shipwrights hammer acacia planks while rope-makers twist papyrus cordage. New vessels take shape beside sailcloth weavers' busy workshops.
Sidon's breakwater shelters merchant galleys laden with glassware and textiles. Dockworkers chant while hoisting amphorae filled with wine and oil.
Papyrus skiffs dart between cargo barges at Thebes riverport. Workers unload Nubian gold and incense while temple accountants record each precious shipment.
Minoan traders haggle over saffron and copper ingots on Zakros wharf. Brightly painted ships bob in crystalline waters below white-plastered port buildings.
Phoenician colonists anchor at Utica's new harbor. Cedar planks, pottery, and bronze tools pile on beaches where trading posts will soon root.
Akkadian cargo ships unload dates and barley at Ur's canal port. Merchants shout in Sumerian while workers haul copper ingots toward temple storehouses.
Egyptian feluccas crowd Abydos landing as priests supervise arrival of granite blocks. Painted barges carry festival offerings down the sacred Nile's current.
Phoenician galleys offload Cypriot copper at Arwad's circular harbor. Metalworkers' furnaces glow orange while traders negotiate beneath stone archways.
Harappan vessels dock at Dholavira's tidal basin. Merchants display lapis lazuli and etched seals while dockworkers transfer jars of precious oils.
Byblos quayside bustles as Egyptian gold trades for mountain cedar. Scribes mark papyrus tallies beside ships preparing the return voyage to Memphis.
Minoan sailors secure purple-sailed ships at Kommos beach. Fishermen mend nets while merchants unload Cypriot pottery onto sun-warmed limestone quays.
Phoenician Tyre's island harbor swarms with dye-workers rinsing murex shells. Cargo galleys from distant coasts await berths along crowded stone jetties.
Mesopotamian reed boats cluster at Eridu's marshy port. Fishermen sell catches while temple barges load offerings bound for Enki's sacred shrine.
Dilmun merchants transfer Indus carnelian to Sumerian vessels. Pearl baskets and date shipments exchange hands on Bahrain's crossroads docks.
Egyptian shipbuilders caulk seams with pitch at Avaris harbor. Workers stack Lebanese timber while new Nile barges await their painted reed sails.
Phoenician Sidon's anchorage fills with merchant crews shouting in many tongues. Dockworkers unload tin ingots beside stacked cedar destined for Egypt's temples.
Minoan Mochlos harbor gleams with fishing boats and trading vessels. Women carry octopus-ware amphorae while sailors repair ropes on painted cargo ships.
Phoenician galley captains supervise linen sail repairs at Byblos while dockside smiths forge bronze fittings. Cedar fragrance drifts across crowded anchorage.
